subject: [PATCH v2 04/14] mm: add VM_UFFD_RWP VMA flag

reviews.author: SeongJae Park
reviews.summary:
Reviewer suggested reordering the 'Signed-off-by' and 'Assisted-by' lines in the patch header, considering it would be more logical to place 'Assisted-by' before 'Signed-off-by'.

The reviewer suggested alternative abbreviations for the VM_UFFD_RWP flag, initially proposing 'u'serfaultfd 'r'ead-protect, then considering 'uf' or 'up', but ultimately deemed it too trivial to matter.

Reviewer suggested that adding the VM_UFFD_RWP VMA flag configuration should be done in conjunction with the introduction of UFFDIO_RWPROTECT, rather than as a separate step.

Reviewer noted that the VM_UFFD_RWP flag should be defined in a more centralized location, such as mmflags.h, to avoid duplication and make it easier to manage
reviews.raw_body:
A silly but loud thought.  Would it make more sense to put Signed-off-by: after
Assisted-by: ?

---

Yet another silly but loud thought.  My first feeling on this was that this
reads like 'u'serfaultfd 'r'ead-protect.  And was further thinking 'uf' for
just 'u'seffaultfd 'f'ault or 'up' for 'u'serfault-'p'rotect might make sense.
But ended up thinking this is too trivial and ain't really matter.

[...]

---

Seems UFFDIO_RWPROTECT will be introduced later.  Would it make more sense to
add this config together with the patch?

---

None of my comments is a blocker.

Reviewed-by: SeongJae Park <sj@kernel.org>


Thanks,
SJ

2026-05-14.patch_summary: This patch adds a new VMA flag, VM_UFFD_RWP, to enable userfaultfd read-write protection (RWP). RWP extends the existing write-protection mechanism to also trap read accesses through userfaultfd. The flag is only meaningful when both PAGE_NONE and uffd PTE bit primitives exist. A new Kconfig symbol, CONFIG_USERFAULTFD_RWP, auto-selects when certain conditions are met. The patch includes changes to various files, including mm.h, proc.rst, task_mmu.c, userfaultfd_k.h, trace/events/mmflags.h, and Kconfig.

reviews.author: Kiryl Shutsemau (author)
reviews.summary:
The author is unsure about the ordering of VM_UFFD_MINOR and VM_UFFD_RWP in the git log, but has decided to keep the current order.

The author is addressing a concern about naming conventions for new VM flags, specifically whether 'ur' is a good choice to distinguish read-write protection (RWP) from write protection (WP). The author agrees that the proposed name 'ur' follows a consistent pattern and will keep it.
reviews.raw_body:
I am not sure. I see it both ways in git log. And I don't have strong
opinion on the order here. Keeping as is.

---

I will keep it. It's "u" + the first distinguishing letter of the mode
name. r distinguishes RWP from WP cleanly.

2026-05-22.patch_summary: This patch adds a new VMA flag, VM_UFFD_RWP, to enable userfaultfd read-write protection (RWP). RWP extends the existing write-protection mechanism to also trap read accesses through userfaultfd. The flag is only meaningful when both PAGE_NONE and uffd PTE bit primitives exist. A new Kconfig symbol, CONFIG_USERFAULTFD_RWP, auto-selects when certain conditions are met. The patch includes changes to various files, including mm.h, proc.rst, task_mmu.c, userfaultfd_k.h, trace/events/mmflags.h, and Kconfig.
